report: Secure Firewall depth + Cisco Security Cloud observability (V6-V8, C6/C9, C12-C16)
verdict_note: All 13 cases PASS. Read-only run (FMC/Splunk GET + SPL search only). Two non-blocking observations: FTDv health yellow (cosmetic Cloud-Connector warning, air-gapped) and a bug in run_report.py's smoke-timeout handler (bytes+str) that aborts the --smoke gate; the offline gate (ruff+pytest) is clean and live smoke was evidenced directly via the MCP read tools.

FMCv.role: Mgmt Center — SDA-ACP + SDA-Decrypt + eStreamer source (TCP 8302)

FTDv.health: yellow (cosmetic: Cloud Connector — Check Cloud Eventing Subscription)

SPLUNK.role: SIEM — Cisco Security Cloud app + Secure Firewall dashboard

HOST1.role: CAMPUS fabric endpoint (traffic source; no Internet)
HOST1.note: pyats console 1-command lag this session -> FMC/Splunk-side evidence preferred

EDGE1.role: cat9000v fabric edge; edge-auth wedge cleared by a reload

cases.objective: Cisco Security Cloud app installed/enabled; Cisco_Security data model + secure_firewall_dashboard view present

evidence.app: CiscoSecurityCloud version 4.0.0, disabled=false, visible=true, label 'Cisco Security Cloud', sharing global
evidence.datamodel: GET data/models/Cisco_Security -> entry name 'Cisco_Security', modelName 'Cisco_Security', Secure_Firewall_Dataset with child datasets Connection_Events/Intrusion_Events/File_Events/Malware_Events
evidence.view: GET data/ui/views/secure_firewall_dashboard -> label 'Secure Firewall Dashboard', isDashboard=true, disabled=false; panels bind tstats over Cisco_Security.Secure_Firewall_Dataset.*

cases.objective: Secure Firewall dashboard fed by dedicated FTD syslog input (UDP 5514)

evidence.input: udp/5514 disabled=false, index=network, sourcetype=cisco:ftd:syslog, connection_host=ip (udp/514 stays cisco:ios for the fabric switches)
evidence.fmc_syslog_server: FTD platform-settings syslog server on port 5514 (re-pointed from 514), src FTD outside .82
evidence.events: index=network sourcetype=cisco:ftd:* -> cisco:ftd:syslog 508 (last 2026-07-18 02:04:40), cisco:ftd:connection 395, cisco:ftd:connection:security 60, cisco:ftd:file 8, cisco:ftd:intrusion 19, cisco:ftd:malware 6 (index-time sub-classification by FTD msg-id works)

cases.objective: EDGE1 reload cleared the wedged Closed-Auth edge-auth; HOST1 services restored (live FTD traffic logged)

evidence.live_ftd_from_data_ip: index=network host=198.18.128.82 sourcetype=cisco:ftd:* -> cisco:ftd:connection 217 (last 2026-07-18 01:41:07), cisco:ftd:connection:security 60, cisco:ftd:intrusion 8, cisco:ftd:syslog 508 (last 02:04:40 ~ run time)
evidence.interpretation: recent live connection/syslog from the FTD data interface proves the SDA east-west path is up (EDGE1 Vlan1021 SVI up, HOST1 MAB re-authorized) and HOST1 traffic transits + is logged by the FTD

cases.objective: eStreamer input enabled; real FMC events flowing; Cisco_Security child datasets populated

evidence.input: CiscoSecurityCloud_sbg_fw_estreamer_input 'SDA_FMC_eStreamer' disabled=false, fmc_host=198.18.128.80, fmc_port=8302, sourcetype=cisco:sfw:estreamer, index=network, estreamer_import_time_range=all_firewall_event_data, event_types=*,connection_log,file_log,intrusion_log,intrusion_packet_log
evidence.events_by_type: index=network sourcetype=cisco:sfw:estreamer -> ConnectionEvent 211, FileEvent 11, IntrusionEvent 8, IntrusionPacket 8 (last 2026-07-18 01:41:07)
evidence.tstats_datasets: Secure_Firewall_Dataset 658; Connection_Events 606; Intrusion_Events 27; File_Events 25; Malware_Events 17 (all child datasets count>0)

cases.objective: Real IPS intrusion event -> signature 'LOCAL C6 RTC test trigger'

evidence.search: index=network sourcetype=cisco:sfw:estreamer EventType=IntrusionEvent by signature -> 'LOCAL C6 RTC test trigger' count 6 (last 2026-07-18 01:01:27); bonus 'POLICY-OTHER eicar test string download attempt' count 2

cases.objective: Real malware file event -> eicar.com, SHA_Disposition=Malware, ThreatName=EICAR

evidence.search: index=network sourcetype=cisco:sfw:estreamer EventType=FileEvent by FileName -> eicar.com count 3, SHA_Disposition=Malware, ThreatName=EICAR (last 2026-07-18 00:59:53); plus mal1-8.com EICAR variants

evidence.rule: C12-Block-Malware-URL @ ruleIndex 6, action BLOCK, urls.urlCategoriesWithReputation = 'Malware Sites' (abba9b63-bb10-4729-b901-2e2aa0f01001), src net-campus10, enabled=true
evidence.note: config-present + deployed; live external hit deferred (HOST1 has no Internet)

evidence.rule: C13-Block-HTTP-App @ ruleIndex 2, action BLOCK, application HTTP (id 676), src net-campus10 -> dst host-splunk, enableSyslog=true, enabled=true
evidence.prior_proof: HOST1 wget http://198.18.128.51:8000/ times out (blocked by app-ID, not port)
evidence.side_effect: this rule blocks CAMPUS->Splunk HTTP on HOST1's upload path — known/expected, not a defect (eStreamer pulls from FMC, unaffected)

evidence.rule: C15-Block-Country-China @ ruleIndex 5, action BLOCK, destinationNetworks Country China (id 156), src net-campus10, enabled=true
evidence.note: config-present + deployed; live external hit deferred (no Internet)

cases.objective: Selective TLS-decryption bypass above the resign rule

evidence.rule: C16-DND-ISE @ ruleIndex 1, ruleAction DO_NOT_DECRYPT, src net-campus10 -> dst net-ise, logging logEnd+sendEvents, enabled=true
evidence.sits_above: Decrypt-CAMPUS-443 @ ruleIndex 2 (DECRYPT_RESIGN, cert SDA-Decrypt-Resign-CA, dst tcp/443)
evidence.prior_proof: openssl s_client HOST1->198.18.134.35:443 shows issuer CN=Mitchcloud-Lab-Root-CA (ISE real cert = bypassed) vs CN=SDA-Decrypt-Resign-CA for other 443

evidence.ftd: deploymentStatus=DEPLOYED, managementState=MANAGED, isConnected=true, accessPolicy=SDA-ACP, licenses ESSENTIALS/MALWARE_DEFENSE/IPS/URL, snortEngine SNORT3

cases.objective: Offline automated gate (ruff + pytest) across the six MCP repos

evidence.unit: 134 passed / 0 failed (cml-mcp 33, ise-mcp 49, firepower-mcp 6, windows-mcp 15, splunk-mcp 21, wlc-mcp 10)
evidence.note: the --smoke gate aborted on a run_report.py handler bug (TimeoutExpired bytes+str) -> O2; live smoke evidenced directly via the MCP read tools instead

observations.text: FTDv healthStatus=yellow — single module warning 'Cloud Connector -> Check Cloud Eventing Subscription'. Expected in an air-gapped lab (no SSE/cloud eventing). Device is DEPLOYED/MANAGED/connected with nothing pending; not a defect in the systems under test. (fmc_list_devices momentarily showed red; the authoritative device record + health roll-up report yellow.)

Test Reports/run_report.py aborts the --smoke gate: its TimeoutExpired handler does (e.stdout or '') + (e.stderr or '') where those are bytes -> TypeError bytes+str, crashing the whole run when any live smoke exceeds the 90s timeout. Offline gate is unaffected. Remediation brief -> harness owner (main session).

reversibility: read-only run — every FMC and Splunk call was a GET or SPL search; no built configuration modified, no round-trips required.
